client_intake.operating_model: make-to-stock distribution from a central warehouse to regional branches

known_systems.name: ERP
known_systems.role: system of record for stock and orders

stated_pain_points.description: Frequent stockouts on fast-moving SKUs despite reported stock on hand
Physical counts routinely disagree with the ERP

urgency.business_trigger: an external stock-count audit flagged material discrepancies

client_intake.initial_scope_hypothesis: Inventory record accuracy is being eroded by uncontrolled adjustments and unreconciled branch processes; focus the assessment on counting discipline and adjustment controls.
client_intake.first_billing_tranche_objective: Deliver an evidence-based initial assessment that quantifies record-accuracy issues and identifies quick wins, positioning a scoped next-phase remediation engagement.

blockers: ERP export access pending IT approval
assessment_readiness.notes: Site walk-around can proceed while data access is arranged.

client_intake.consultant_notes: Sponsor is motivated by the audit finding; move quickly on the walk-around to build early evidence.

known_systems.name: ERP
known_systems.role: system of record for stock, purchasing, and orders

known_systems.name: branch reorder spreadsheets
known_systems.notes: one per branch, maintained manually, not integrated with the ERP

records_source_of_truth.notes: Nominally the ERP, but staff trust physical counts more, and branches rely on their own spreadsheets for reordering.

integrations.description: Branch spreadsheets are reconciled to the ERP by manual re-keying

manual_workarounds.description: Branches maintain separate reorder spreadsheets because they do not trust ERP availability figures

data_quality_concerns.description: On-hand balances diverge from physical counts across many SKUs
Negative on-hand balances are not flagged or prevented by the ERP

access_status.notes: ERP read access requested and pending IT approval; branch spreadsheets restricted pending management sign-off.

inventory_system_profile.consultant_notes: Multiple competing sources of truth is a core theme; confirm once ERP export is available.

evidence_references.summary: Operations manager reported frequent stockouts on fast-moving SKUs even when the ERP shows stock on hand, and said purchasing no longer trusts on-hand figures.
evidence_references.confidence_notes: Sponsor's account; consistent with the audit trigger but not yet quantified from data.
evidence_references.access_notes: Captured in intake-call notes; no recording retained.
evidence_references.consultant_notes: Quantify stockout frequency once the ERP export is available.

evidence_references.summary: Warehouse lead reported that physical cycle counts 'almost always' disagree with the ERP, and that discrepancies are resolved by manually adjusting the ERP to match the count without root-cause review.
evidence_references.confidence_notes: First-hand account from the person performing counts; not yet corroborated against count records.
evidence_references.access_notes: Verbal statement captured in interview notes; no recording retained.
evidence_references.retention_notes: Interview notes held by consultant; formal retention policy to be defined in a later phase.
evidence_references.consultant_notes: Corroborate against the last cycle-count report and the adjustment log once ERP access is granted.

evidence_references.summary: Photographs of small-parts bin zones showing faded/missing location labels and bins holding mixed SKUs without partition labels.
evidence_references.confidence_notes: Direct photographic evidence taken during the walk-around.
evidence_references.access_notes: Image files retained by consultant; not embedded here.
evidence_references.consultant_notes: Strong support for the bin-labeling quick win.

stated_pain_points.description: Counts rarely match the ERP, and there is no time to investigate why

process_claims.claim: Cycle counts are done weekly but only on a subset of aisles chosen informally
Received goods are sometimes put away before being booked into the ERP

system_claims.claim: The ERP does not flag negative on-hand balances, so they go unnoticed

quantified_impacts.description: Time spent reconciling counts each week

contradictions_or_followups.description: Operations manager stated counts are full-warehouse monthly; warehouse lead describes partial weekly. Reconcile the actual counting cadence.
Obtain the adjustment log to quantify unreviewed adjustments.

stakeholder_interviews.consultant_notes: Credible, hands-on account. Prioritize getting the adjustment log to corroborate.

visual_observations.description: Multiple small-parts bins had faded or missing location labels, and several bins held mixed SKUs without partition labels.
visual_observations.operational_implication: Ambiguous bin identity makes accurate picking and counting difficult, contributing to the reported count-vs-ERP discrepancies.
visual_observations.suggested_follow_up: Relabel affected bin zones and confirm one-SKU-per-location discipline; sample-count a labeled vs unlabeled zone to compare accuracy.
visual_observations.consultant_notes: Photographed several bins; this looks like a strong quick-win candidate.

workflow_observations.current_state: When a cycle count disagrees with the ERP, staff adjust the ERP on-hand quantity to match the count directly, with no approval step and no recorded reason code.
workflow_observations.observed_gap: Inventory adjustments lack review, reason codes, and root-cause analysis, so recurring accuracy problems are masked rather than corrected.
workflow_observations.business_impact: Persistent record inaccuracy drives stockouts on fast movers and undermines trust in ERP stock figures for purchasing and finance.

control_risk.description: Unrestricted, unreviewed adjustments are a segregation-of-duties and shrink-concealment risk.

workflow_observations.consultant_notes: Introducing an approval threshold and mandatory reason codes is a plausible quick win; confirm ERP supports reason codes.

packet_metadata.source_note: Fictional, anonymized example engagement for illustration and testing.

validation_notes.note: All nested evidence_references resolve within this packet (evid_alpha_001, evid_alpha_002, evid_alpha_003).
All nested objects reference intake_alpha_2026 as related_intake_id.
ERP data export still pending; several claims remain to be corroborated against system data.
